name: Arena POWERSKIN VELOCE
description: Arena's new racer runs on Hyperforce, their first tense-elastic fabric, chasing a fit swimmers actually want: compression without the shrink-wrap feeling. Early word is it moves like a training suit and squeezes like a tech suit, a combination that usually costs you one or the other.

name: Arena Cobra Ultra Swipe goggles
description: fog mid-swim, wipe the lens with a finger, done. Anti-fog that resets itself instead of quitting on you at 50 meters in.

name: FORM Smart Swim 2
description: the HeadCoach algorithm got sharper this year. It'll actually catch you riding too high on freestyle.

name: THEMAGIC5 Vector
description: scan your face from your couch, a robot cuts your gasket. Custom-fit goggles, no fitting room required.

name: FINIS Swimmer's Snorkel
description: the default center-mount snorkel on every pool deck. Doesn't leak, stays in place, and lets you fix your stroke instead of fighting for air.

name: FINIS Cardio Cap Snorkel
description: a restrictor cap that turns the Swimmer's Snorkel into a lung workout. Up to 40% more work on your breathing without changing a single set.

name: Arena Swim Snorkel III
description: the tank of the category. Survives seasons of chlorine abuse, seals leak-free, and the liquid-silicone strap stays comfortable for the long haul.

name: MP Michael Phelps Focus Snorkel
description: low-profile, hydrodynamic, and rock-stable even at full speed with fins. Built for swimmers chasing a perfectly quiet head position.

name: Speedo Center-Mount Snorkel
description: the low-risk way into snorkel training. Comfortable head mount, does the core job for half the price of the premium picks.

name: Speedo Team Kickboard
description: the practice-deck standard. Contoured EVA foam with underside grooves and gripped edges so your hands stay parked while you kick your brains out.

name: Sporti Pull Buoy
description: the workhorse buoy. Figure-eight EVA sits between the thighs, lifts the hips, and shuts the legs off so a set is all pull and body line. Cheap, indestructible, in every team bag.

name: Arena Powerfin Pro II
description: a short-blade silicone fin built for tempo, not cruising. The open heel frees the ankle and the slotted blade keeps your kick fast instead of turning it into a slow-motion flutter.

name: FINIS Tempo Trainer Pro
description: a waterproof metronome that tucks under your cap and beeps your target pace. Hold stroke rate or send-off on the beep and pacing stops being a guess.

name: FINIS Agility Paddles
description: strapless paddles that stay on only when your catch is right. Drop the elbow or slip the hand and they fall off mid-stroke, so they coach your pull instead of just loading it.

name: TYR Catalyst Training Paddles
description: a contoured power paddle with adjustable tubing that grips the whole hand. Extra surface area for strength sets without the flat-plate slap of the old rectangular paddles.

name: Speedo Vanquisher 2.0 Goggles
description: the low-profile training goggle that has outlasted every trend. Anti-fog coating, four nose-bridge sizes in the box, and a fit that disappears once you push off.

name: TYR Big Mesh Mummy Backpack
description: the mesh bag that swallows a full deck's worth of buoys, paddles, fins and snorkels, then drains and dries them on the walk to the car instead of stewing in a puddle.
